title: Mizoram Police chuan a hnuaia mi ang hian thuchhuah an siam:
content:
567/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 16th May, 2026 :YouTube channel thenkhatah mipat-hmeichhiatna lam hawi thawnthu ngialngan tak tak chhiar chhuah thin a nih thu a dawnin, Cyber Crime Police Station chuan a chhuizui nghal a. Chutah YouTube channel pahnih, “@NGAITHLASUHPUITLINGHUANG (PUITLUNG HUANG)” leh “@puitlinghuangzz (NAUPANGTIAMLOH)” te chuan Mizo tawngin hetiang thawnthu phuahchawp chhiar chhuahna video tam tak an upload tih hmuhchhuah a ni.

Heng video-te hi mipui en theih tura chhawpchhuah an ni a, mipuite rilru hruai khawlovin, a bik takin naupangte rilru a tihbuai theih avangin heng an thiltihte hi Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 leh Information Technology Act, 2000 hnuaia hrem theih a ni a. Cyber Crime Police Station chuan Case No.16/26 dt. 26.04.2026 u/s 294 BNS r/w 67 IT Act 2000 chu register nghalin, investigation pawh rang takin a kalpui nghal a ni.

YouTube puihna leh Investigating Officer-in technical investigation a tihte kaltlangin, account neitute chu ni 14.05.2026 khan hriatchhuah an ni ta a. Youtube account @NGAITHLASUHPUITLINGHUANG tih chu Henry Lalrinpuia (29) S/o Lalhmangaiha, Hunthar, Aizawl siam leh khawih niin @puitlinghuangzz tih chu Vanlalhmangaihzuala (35) S/o Vanlalhunga(L), Chaltui, Phuldungsei siam leh khawih a ni thung. Police hmalaknain an video-te mipui en theih lova dah a ni a, pawikhawih tute hi dan hnuaia hrem turin man an niin an mobile phone hman laite chu evidence atan man sak an ni bawk. Henry Lalrinpuia (29) S/o Lalhmangaiha, Hunthar, Aizawl hi ni 15.5.2026 ah man niin, Vanlalhmangaihzuala (35) S/o Vanlalhunga(L), Chaltui, Phuldungsei hi ni 16.5.2026 ah man a ni thung.

Mizoram Police chuan social media leh online platform dangah te hetiang mipat-hmeichhiatna lam hawi thlalak, video leh thawnthute thehdarh chu dan kalh a ni tih hre turin mipuite a chah nawn leh a. Social media hmanga dan bawhchhiate chungah na takin Mizoram Police chuan action a la zel dawn a ni.

title: UBI REGIONAL MANAGER-IN CM HNENAH KHAWZAWL DISTRICT HOSPITAL WATER FILTER LEINA HLAN
content:
566/2026-2027

Khawzawl the 16th May 2026 :Vawiina Chief Minister-in Samthang atanga Aizawl a panna lamah Khawzawlah a chawl a, Khawzawlah hian Dr. Suvajit Samanta, Regional Head, UBI, Jorhat chuan an bank Khawzawl Branch-in an hlep atanga an CSR Rs. 83,400 chu Khawzawl District Hospital-a Water Filter leina atan a lo hlan.

UBI hian an Branch kum khat hlep atanga 1% hi CSR atana pek theihna dan an nei a. Tun kum kalta a Khawzawl Branch hlep hian District Hospital mamawh ang leina hi daih chiah lo mah se staff te thahnemngaihna leh mipui mamawhna a san avangin hetiang zat hi an pe ta a ni.

title: MLA Speaker Pu Lalbiakzama chuan Hmar Martarte Ni vawi 36-na a hmanpui
content:
565/2026-2027

16th May, 2026:Vawiin khan Hmar Martarte Ni a vawi 36-na chu Manipur-a Churachandpur Saikawt Playground-ah hman a ni a, Speaker Pu Lalbiakzama chuan khuallian niin a hmanpui a, he hunah hian a vawi 19-na atan Hmar Martyr's Trophy khelh bawk a ni. Hmar mipuite hnenah thuchah sawiin Speaker chuan Hmar Martarte inhlanna chu hnam rohlu leh hriatreng tlak a ni a, Martarte thisen hian hnam nunna, zahawmna leh inpumkhatna a siam, a ti.

Speaker chuan ram leh hnam humhalhna atan an nunna hlantu Martarte chu chawimawina sang takin chibai buk a. Martarte inhlanna chu thuin sawi kim theih a ni lo va, an thisen leh an inhlanna hian hnam nunna, zahawmna leh inpumkhatna lungphum a siam tih a sawi. Martarte chu hriatrengna mai an ni lova, hnam ro hlu an ni a, an inhlanna hian thangtharte thinlungah huaisenna, inpumkhatna leh ram hmangaihna a tuh zel tih a sawi bawk.

Speaker chuan Hmar hnam leh Mizo identity inzawmna pawimawhna a sawi chhuak a, Hmar hruaitute leh organization ten Mizo/Zo inpumkhatna an ngai pawimawh chu lawmawm a ti a. Manipur-a Hmar organization ten Mizo/Zo identity reaffirmation an tih chu Zo hnahthlakte inpumkhatna leh hnam nunphung humhalhna kawngah pawimawh tak a ni tih a sawi.

Mizoram sorkar leh HPC-D inkara remna thuthlung tluantling siam a ni tawh chu lawmawm a tih thu Pu Lalbiakzama chuan a sawi a. April ni 30, 2026-a HPC-D ten ralthuam an tuk chu Mizorama remna leh muanna dinnghehna tur atan hmasawnna pawimawh tak a ni, a ti a. Ralthuam kenga rammu a tawp ta hi a lawmawm thu leh he remna hian inpumkhatna leh hmasawnna a tihchak dawn thu a sawi bawk.

Manipur-a chi leh chi buaina chungchangah Speaker chuan Zofa unau ten harsatna leh tawrhna nasa tak an tuar mek chu a lungngaihthlak a tih thu a sawi a, Zofa/Mizo zawng zawngte chu inpumkhatna leh inunauna vawng nung zel tura ngenin, tawng hrang leh identity hrang nei mah se Mizo/Zo identity chuan min khungkhawm vek tih hre reng tur leh mahni culture leh identity vawng chungin inpumkhat zel turin a chah a ni.

A thu tawpna a tan Speaker chuan  thangtharte chu Martarte nunin min zirtir angin huaisenna, rinawmna, thawhrimna, thuhnuairawlhna, mi dang hmangaihna leh ram leh hnam tana inhlanna nei turin a fuih a. Hmar Martarte thisen hian Zofa zawng zawngte chu remna, zahawmna leh hmasawnna kawngah min hruai zel a beisei, a ti.

Vawiin programme-ah hian Rev.Timothy Lalzuimawii'n ṭawngtaina neiin hun ṭan a ni a, Martarte hriatrengna atan tawngtaina hun Pi Lalrothar chuan a hmang bawk. EFCI, Saikot Presbytery Superintendent, Rev. Lunzamanga'n tawngtaiin hun a khar.

He Programme-ah hian Pu V. Malsawmtluanga, MLA, Vice Chairman leh High Power Committee member te, Hmar Inpui leh Hmartob hruaitu te, Martar chhungkua te, Pu Ricky Lalremsanga, CEM of Shinlung Hills Council, Council Member Pu Lalvenhima Hmar leh Pu Vanlalsiamsanga te bakah mipui tam tak an tel a ni.

Attachment

title: SAINIK SCHOOL CHHINGCHHIP CELEBRATES 10TH FOUNDER’S DAY; MR. TBC LALVENCHUNGA (MLA) ATTENDS AS CHIEF GUEST
content:
88/2026-2027

Dated Serchhip, the 16th May 2026:Sainik School Chhingchhip celebrated its 10th Founder’s Day today at the School Assembly Hall and Parade Ground in the presence of Chief Guest Mr. TBC Lalvenchunga, MLA and Advisor (Finance & Planning) to the Chief Minister of Mizoram. The landmark institutional event was also attended by Guest of Honor Brigadier Pushpendar Sorayan, Commander of the 23 Sector, Assam Rifles, alongside senior civil dignitaries, military officers, ex-students, and parents.

Speaking on the occasion, the Chief Guest exhorted the cadets to apply themselves single-mindedly to their studies, emphasizing that knowledge has become the most vital asset in today’s world. He encouraged the students to actively participate in games, sports, and co-curricular activities to nurture a well-rounded personality. Mr. TBC Lalvenchunga further urged the people of the state to fully utilize the premier institution by admitting their wards to groom them into future leaders of the nation.

The program included a welcome address from Mrs. Zirsangpuii, PGT English, and the presentation of the Annual Report by Principal Col. Soumyabrata Dhar highlighting academic and sports achievements. The celebration featured a vibrant cultural show, including an entertaining solo song, traditional Mizo fusion group dances, Rawkhatlak, and a patriotic dance. During the prize-giving ceremony, outstanding cadets received book prizes and certificates, with the Mahendra Nath Mulla (Green) House lifting the coveted Best in Academics Trophy. The grand event culminated with a Science and Arts Exhibition showcasing the innovative projects and creative talents of the cadets.

Attachment

title: ADVISER TO CM (FINANCE, PLANNING) PU TBC LALVENCHHUNGA'N SAINIK  SCHOOL CHHINGCHHIP '10TH FOUNDER'S DAY'  LAWMNAAH KHUALLIAN NIIN HUN A HMANPUI
content:
564/2026-2027

Dated Serchhip, the 16th May 2026:Vawiin khan Sawrkar laipui Minsitry of Defence hnuaia zirna kalpui mekna hmun pakhat  Sainik School, Chhingchhip chuan '10th Founder's Day' an hmang a, Adviser to CM Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga chuan khuallian niin hun a hmanpui a, Brigadier Pushpendar Sorayan, Commander of the 23 Sector, Assam Rifles pawhin Khualzahawm niin a telpui bawk a ni.

Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga chuan Sainik School, Chhingchhip-in an school din tan ni vawi  sawmna  '10th Founder's Day' an thleng chu a lawmpui thu sawiin, he school-a zir mek zirlai/cadets-te chu an theihna zawng zawng nena an zirlaite bei tur leh mahni inthununna  (Self-discipline) vawng  chunga  tha taka zir chhuak theuh turin a  fuih a. Nu leh pate pawh an fate tana theihtawp chhuahpui zel tura fuihin, tun CBSE Academics Session liam taa Sainik School, Chhingchhip  atanga subject hrang hranga zirlai ti thate hnenah  chawimawina a hlan nghal bawk a ni.

He hunah hian Sainik School, Chhingchhip  Principal  Col. Soumyabrata Dhar chuan sawm bikte leh zirlai nu leh pa kalkhawmte  lawmna thu sawiin, kum 2025-2026 chhunga  Sainik School chet velna 'Annual Report' tlangpui a sawi a; Sainik School Chhingchhip  Vice Principal Lt. Col Kartik Sood chuan lawmthu sawiin programme hi a khar a ni.

10th Founder's Day pual hian Sainik School zirlaite atangin Cultural Programme leh Special Items hmuhnawm leh ngaihnawm tak takte thlir ho a nih bakah 'Arts and Crafts & Science Stream' lam hawi  Exhibition ropui tak buatsaih a ni bawk a ni.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER ATTENDED GINGER FESTIVAL
content:
87/2026-2027

Samthang, 16th May 2026 :Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma today attended the Ginger Festival organised by the local community at Samthang Community Hall in Champhai district.

He stressed the need to gradually promote organic ginger cultivation and strengthen ginger processing and value addition in Mizoram. He said the government is making steady preparations in this direction.

Speaking at the programme, the Chief Minister said Mizoram ginger is of high quality, suitable for medicinal use, rich in essential oils and low in fibre. In view of its potential, the state government secured approval from the Ministry of DoNER for the “Mizoram Ginger USP – Sustainable Cultivation & Value Chain Development Project” worth around Rs. 190 crore. Of this, Rs. 30.13 crore has been sanctioned for setting up ginger processing units.

He stated that the main processing unit will be established at Mualkawi in Champhai district, with supporting units planned at Tualcheng, Vaphai and Zotlang, benefitting farmers across the region.

The Chief Minister emphasised that sustainable and organic farming practices are essential for improving the ginger market while protecting the soil. He also highlighted the importance of processing and value addition, noting that selling raw ginger alone results in lower returns and wastage.

Dr. KC Lalmalsawmzauva, Adviser to the Chief Minister, said the success of ginger procurement was largely due to the hard work of farmers from Samthang and surrounding areas. He added that the government has already spent around Rs. 18 crore on support price procurement this year.

The programme was chaired by Pu Lianzika, Organising Chairman, with welcome remarks delivered by Pu Lalchungnunga, VCP. Pu Lalparliana, President of the Ginger Association, also addressed the gathering.

Attachment

title: SAWHTHING HI ORGANIC ZAWNGA KAN KALPUI TIAL TIAL A NGAI A, A SAWNGBAWLNA PAWH KAN MAMAWH A. SORKAR A INBUATSAIH CHHO ZEL.– Lalduhoma, Chief Minister
content:
563/2026-2027

Samthang, the 16th May 2026 :Vawiin khan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan Samthang Community Hall-ah khawtlang mipuite buatsaih Sawhthing Kut a hmanpui a, Sawhthing hi kan ram leilung tichhe tam lo zawng leh a hralhna lama kan lo hlawkpui zawk theih nan Organic kan uar a tul a. A processing kan kalpui a tul bawk a. Kan inbuatsaih chho zel, a ti.

Samthang sawhthing kutah hian Dr. KC Lalmalsawmzauva chu khualzahawm a ni.

Thusawiin Chief Minister chuan, Ka politics zin kawng leh sawhthing market hian inanna tak a nei a, a kawng a bum boh a. Mahse, Pathian hun ruat lo thleng hi tu ma dan rual a ni lo. Nikumah inzir chawpin hma kan la a, harsatna tam tak kan tawk nain kan pek chhuah tur Support Price zawng zawng kan pe chhuak thei.

Kuminah pawh a tir lamah a rate hi a tha mai lo va. Kan hun tiam thlen hmaa a rate hniama lo hralh ta mai khua an lo awm bawk a. Nimahsela, Pathian zarah kan tum ang ngeiin kan hun tiam atangin kan lei tan leh a ni. Tunah phei chuan kan beisei phak bakin sawhthing rate a rawn sang ta a. Hei hi kan tih a ni lo va engkim chunga roreltu Pathian hnathawh a ni tih kan hria a ni.

Mizoram sawhthing hi a quality a tha a, damdawi anga hman theih a ni a, essential oil a ngah a, fibre a nei tlem si a, Ministry of DoNER-ah Unique Selling Proposition (USP) min kalpui turin kan dil a. Min rawn phalsak ta a.

USP - Sustainable Cultivation & Value Chain Management Project kan siam a. Project Cost hi Rs. 190 Crore vel a ni a. A processing Unit dinna tur bik atan Rs. 30.13. Crore kan ruahman a. Tun Nilaini liam ta khan a project hawnna hi online-in DoNER Minister nen kan nei a, a hawnna programme kan hman zawh rual hian Processing Unit kan dinna tur bik sum Rs. 30.13 Crore hi an rawn sanction nghal a ni. Hetiang taka pawisa sanction rang hi sawi tur a vang khawp mai.

Champhai district hi Sawhthing kan thar tamna district a nih angin Mualkawiah Processing Unit pui ber din a, a chawmtu tur a tenau deuh zawk pathum Tualcheng, Vaphai leh Zotlangah din kan rilruk a. He lai bial mipui zawng zawngten in chhawr phak vek dawn a ni.

Sawhthing market a that tial tial theih nan sawhthing hrisel kan thar a ngai a, kan ram leilung a tihchhiat nasat loh nan sustainable cultivation kan kalpui a ngai a. Chumi lam hawi zawnga kal hret hret a, engtikah emaw chuan Organic state-a inpuan kan duh a ni. A pulunga kan hralh ringawt hian bo thlau a tam thin a, a hlawk tawk lo va. A sawngbawlna hi hmun hrang hrangah neih chhoh zel kan tum, a ti.

Khaw taima, Sawhthing, fur Zikhlum leh Samtawk tharchhuak nasa in ni hi a chhuanawm ka ti a. Tam lehzual in tharchhuah zel theih nan duhsakna ka hlan che u a, theihtawp kan chhuah tlang dawn nia, a ti.

Dr. KC Lalmalsawmzauva, Adviser to CM pawhin thusawiin, sorkarin sawhthing a lei atanga chiang taka lo lang chu Samthang leh a chhehvel tlang dungte hi in taima a, in thawkrim tih hi a ni. Mizoram pumah tunah hunah hian nikum aiin kan la hralh chhuak tlem a, kumin bika support price kan pek tawh chin hi Rs. 18 Crore vel a ni tawh a. Kuminah SCC 24 velah Rs. 50 leh a chunglama kan hralh avangin nikum ai chuan Support Price hi kan seng tlem zawkin a rinawm, a ti.

Sawhthing Kut hi Pu Lianzika, Organising Chairmanin a kaihruai a, Pu Lalchungnunga, VCP in kalkhawmte lawmna a nei a, Pu Lalparliana, Sawhthing Association President-in thu a sawi bawk.

Samthangah hian chhungkua 230 vel a cheng mek a, a tam zawk hian lo neiin ei an zawng a, Sawhthing bakah Fur Zikhlum an ching nasa bawk a ni.

Attachment

title: UNION MINISTER OF STATE FOR CONSUMER AFFAIRS, FOOD & PUBLIC DISTRIBUTION VISITS CHAWNGTLAI
content:
86/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 16th May, 2026 :Today, the Union Minister of State for Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya visited the Handloom Training Centre and houses constructed under PMAY-G in Chawngtlai village. She also held an interaction programme with Self Help Group (SHG) members at the VC House. The Minister was accompanied by Khawzawl ADC Pu K. Zorammuana, SDO Pu Chandrakant Bagoria and other district officials.

The Minister interacted with the staff and trainees of the Handloom Training Centre and remarked that the beautifully woven traditional textiles produced there should be promoted on a wider scale. She also visited two households whose homes had been completed under the PMAY-G scheme and expressed her happiness that they were able to build their homes through assistance provided under the government scheme.

During her interaction with SHG members at the VC House, the Minister advised them to ensure purity and maintain good quality in all SHG products so that the public can trust and confidently use them. Highlighting the Central Government’s commitment towards the upliftment and development of women, youth, farmers and economically weaker sections, she encouraged the women of Khawzawl to make full use of the various schemes meant for women. She further stressed the importance of exploring wider marketing avenues and better pricing opportunities for SHG products.

Attachment

title: UNION MINISTER OF STATE FOR CONSUMER AFFAIRS, FOOD & PUBLIC DISTRIBUTION-IN CHAWNGTLAI TLAWH
content:
562/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 16th May, 2026 :Vawiin khan Union Minister of State for Consumer Affairs, Food And Public Distribution Pi Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya chuan Chawngtlai khuaa Handloom Training Centre leh PMAY-G hnuaia insakte tlawhin, SHG member-te nen VC House-ah inkawmna hun an hmang. Minister hi Khawzawl ADC Pu K. Zorammuana, SDO Pu Chandrakant Bagoria leh official dangten an tawiawm a ni.

Minister chuan Handloom Training Centre-a thawktute leh zirlaite nen inkawmin, an puan tah mawi tak takte chu zau zawka pholan theih ni se tha a tih thu a sawi a. PMAY-G hnuaia in sa zo tawh chhungkaw 2 tlawhin, sawrkar scheme hmang tangkaia in an din thei chu a lawmpui thu a sawi a. VC House-ah Chawngtlai SHG member-te nen inkawmna hun an hmannaah Minister chuan mipuiin SHG thil siam chhuah (product) reng reng tha tura an rin theih nan thianghlim tak leh quality tha taka thil siam zel turin a chah a. Sawrkar laipui chuan hmeichhia, thalai, lo neitute leh mi harsa zawkte chawikanna leh tihhmasawn a ngaihpawimawhzia tarlangin, sawrkarin hmeichhiate tana scheme hrang hrang a siamte Khawzawl nuho pawhin hmang tangkai turin a fuih a. SHG thil siam chhuahte pawh zau zawka thehdarh dan leh man tha zawka an hralh theihna turte ngaihtuah a pawimawh thu a sawi.

Attachment
